name: Razorpay API Protection — Redis Rate Limiting
scenario: Protect Razorpay's payment API from abuse. A competitor's scraper is firing 5,000 requests/minute to the payment status endpoint. Real merchants are getting 503 errors. Implement Redis-based rate limiting that allows 100 requests/minute for payment creation and 500/minute for status checks — with 429 responses and Retry-After headers.
description: FixedWindowRateLimiter + Spring Filter + per-endpoint limits + whitelist + RateLimitResult record. MockMvc integration tests verify 429 on limit exceeded and correct Retry-After header.

core.description: RateLimitResult record: allowed, count, limit, remaining, resetInSeconds.

RazorpayRateLimiter @Service: check(merchantId, endpoint) → RateLimitResult. Endpoint limits: /v1/payments POST=100/min, /v1/payments GET=500/min, /v1/refunds=20/min, default=200/min. Redis INCR + EXPIRE(count==1) pattern.

RateLimitFilter @Component @Order(1): extracts X-Merchant-Id header. If missing: IP-based limiting (20/min). Checks rate via RazorpayRateLimiter. On limit: HTTP 429 + Retry-After + JSON body. On allow: X-RateLimit-Remaining header. Never call chain.doFilter after 429.
WhitelistService @Service: addToWhitelist(merchantId), isWhitelisted(merchantId). Redis Set 'razorpay:rate:whitelist'. Filter skips rate check for whitelisted merchants.

MockMvc integration tests (4): (1) requests 1-100 return 200 for /v1/payments POST, (2) request 101 returns 429 with Retry-After, (3) /v1/payments GET allows 500 (separate counter), (4) whitelisted merchant always gets 200. All 4 pass.
application.properties: Redis config. Test uses unique merchantId per test run to prevent interference.

bonus.description: Add MerchantTier (STARTUP=50, GROWTH=200, ENTERPRISE=2000) stored in Redis Hash. Filter reads tier and applies tier-specific base limit scaled by endpoint factor. @SpringBootTest verifying STARTUP blocked at 51, GROWTH at 201.
Add /api/rate-limit/status endpoint returning JSON with requestsThisWindow, limit, remaining, resetInSeconds for the requesting merchant. @SpringBootTest verifying status reflects actual usage.
Sliding window rate limiter alternative: SlidingWindowRateLimiter using ZSet for /v1/payments endpoint. Compare behavior at minute boundary with fixed window. Write test demonstrating the difference.

data_to_use:
Test merchants:
  M001: normal merchant (not whitelisted)
  M002: scraper merchant (not whitelisted)
  M_INTERNAL: whitelisted internal service

Rate limits:
  POST /v1/payments:   100 requests/minute
  GET  /v1/payments:   500 requests/minute
  POST /v1/refunds:     20 requests/minute
  Default:             200 requests/minute

Expected behaviors:
  M001 makes 100 POST /v1/payments → all 200
  M001 makes 101st request → 429 + Retry-After
  M_INTERNAL makes 200 requests → all 200 (whitelisted)
  M002 POST /v1/refunds: 20th = 200, 21st = 429
example_output:
# Normal request (within limit):
HTTP 200 OK
X-RateLimit-Limit: 100
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 53

# Over limit:
HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Retry-After: 23
X-RateLimit-Limit: 100
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0
{"error":"Rate limit exceeded","retryAfterSeconds":23}

# Whitelisted merchant:
HTTP 200 OK  (no X-RateLimit headers, or bypass headers)

# Tests:
✓ first100Requests_return200
✓ request101_returns429WithRetryAfter
✓ getEndpoint_hasSeparateHigherLimit
✓ whitelistedMerchant_neverThrottled
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. EXPIRE only when count==1 — critical: don't call expire on every request or the window never resets while traffic is active. return after 429 in Filter: chain.doFilter must NOT be called after writing 429 — double processing occurs. GET vs POST same path: separate counter keys need method prefix (e.g., 'rate:M001:GET_v1_payments:window' vs 'rate:M001:POST_v1_payments:window'). Whitelist uses Redis Set: SISMEMBER is O(1). MockMvc test isolation: use unique merchantId per test (System.currentTimeMillis()) to prevent cross-test counter contamination.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. All 4 MockMvc tests pass. README includes a 'rate limit policy' table showing limits per endpoint. Test output shown in README.
tips_for_success: Test isolation: 'test:' + UUID or timestamp as merchantId prefix prevents tests sharing counters
Endpoint key: include HTTP method to distinguish POST /v1/payments (100/min) from GET (500/min)
count==1 check: only set TTL on first request; test this explicitly
return after 429: add a comment 'CRITICAL: must return here' to make it obvious
MockMvc @SpringBootTest: requires real Redis; use @EmbeddedKafka-equivalent or test Redis
concepts_reinforced: Redis INCR for atomic distributed counter
Fixed window rate limiting with minute bucket key
HTTP 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header
Per-endpoint rate limits via key granularity
